title: Limitations of ZAFF-AMBER absolute binding free energy calculation for zinc metalloenzyme inhibitors: a replicate-pair analysis on MMP-1
abstract: Absolute binding free energy (ABFE) calculations on zinc metalloenzymes present a stress test for non-bonded zinc force fields such as ZAFF-AMBER: the catalytic Zn binding chemistry of hydroxamate, sulfonamide, and thiol inhibitors must be reproduced from a fixed-charge, restraint-based description of the metal coordination shell. We benchmark the ZAFF-AMBER + GAFF-2 + AM1-BCC ABFE protocol on fourteen ChEMBL MMP-1 inhibitors spanning sub-nanomolar to micromolar potency (4--18000 nM, six scaffold classes including hydroxamate, non-hydroxamate sulfonamide, aryl sulfone, carboxylate, fluoro-aryl), each computed with up to three independent replicates using the openmmtools MultiStateSampler. Our central observation is that reproducibility is not accuracy: replicates of the same compound agree to within 0.06 kcal/mol for some compounds yet are systematically biased by 4--18 kcal/mol from the experimental affinity, while other compounds give catastrophic 18.8 kcal/mol replicate variance whose mean nonetheless lands within 1 kcal/mol of experiment. Four of fourteen compounds sign-flip their predicted binding direction; five over-bind by 8--18 kcal/mol; three high-potency hydroxamates under-bind by 8--10 kcal/mol. We additionally document two pipeline pitfalls — ReplicaExchangeSampler swap-all deadlock at the first exchange iteration and ABFE production NaN crashes on medium-sized ligands without explicit minimization+heating+NPT warmup — and recommend at least 3-replicate ABFE with explicit per-class error budgeting for ZAFF-AMBER metalloenzyme inhibitor ranking. Keywords: ABFE, ZAFF-AMBER, zinc metalloenzyme, MMP-1, hydroxamate, MultiStateSampler, replicate variance, openmmtools.

authors.name: HanCheongWoo

disclaimer: In silico only. No wet-lab data. IRB caveat does not apply (no human subjects).

ai_disclosure.note: Author verified all claims and ran all simulations; AI assisted with prose and code structure only.
